title: Parallel Processing of the Same Transaction
breadcrumbs: Development Considerations for Performance and Scalability
Parallel Processing of the Same Transaction
text: Parallel Processing of the Same Transaction Concurrent transactions carry a serious risk for contention. This is especially so when running the same transaction concurrently, because the same resources are allocated repeatedly. Uniface allows the same transaction to be run concurrently even by the same user process. Because Uniface commit and rollback apply to a complete path, each transaction needs a path for exclusive use. If an instance is created using the Transaction property set to TRUE , a new path, including an external data structure and stepped hitlist, is created for that instance and all instances created from it. This can be quite expensive in terms of resources. Note: This feature applies only to non-modal forms. Related Links commit Commit a transaction to a DBMS or path. rollback Undo the transaction (if supported by DBMS). Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
